data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T233540-1514ca02/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T233540-1514ca02/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T233540-1514ca02/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T233540-1514ca02/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T233540-1514ca02/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-c739d7ec">
    <title>feat(bead): ddx bead ac-check — mechanical per-AC verification tool (additive)</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Three observed cases on 2026-05-10 of fake-work: implementer
  models write aspirational commit messages but the actual diff
  is minimal/absent. The reviewer rubber-stamps the attempt and
  the bead closes with AC not satisfied.

  Cases:
    - ddx-1f774edf "remove network I/O from Land()" — claimed
      PreClaimGitOps interface + LandingGitOps cleanup; diff
      was 4 files / 30 lines.
    - ddx-94a368b6 "conformance transition-matrix coverage" —
      preserved on iteration ref, never landed.
    - ddx-d22293ba "wire RetainDays prune" — claimed prune loop,
      counters, 5 tests, agent-logs/workers scope; diff was 5
      lines setting a default value.

  The reviewer ALREADY emits per_ac[].grade + per_ac[].evidence
  (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:204-234). The
  defect is that the evidence string is UNVERIFIED TEXT. A
  rubber-stamping reviewer can write "evidence": "types.go:42"
  for an AC it never checked. Strengthening reviewer prose
  without a mechanical forcing function would create
  false-positive BLOCKs on legitimate work (renamed tests,
  negative AC, mechanical refactors, etc.) and still fail to
  catch fake-work convincingly.

  This bead is the FIRST of two: it adds a mechanical
  AC-verification tool that both implementer (pre-commit
  self-check) and reviewer (gate ratification) invoke with the
  SAME inputs. Sister bead changes the reviewer behavior to
  ratify this tool's output rather than re-grep the diff
  independently.

CURRENT STATE
  - No CLI subcommand performs per-AC verification mechanically.
  - The reviewer prompt asks for per-AC grading but provides no
    structured input — reviewer must grep a possibly-30k-line
    diff to find evidence, which scales poorly and invites
    rubber-stamping.
  - Implementer has no way to self-check whether its work would
    pass review before committing — pure information
    asymmetry.

PROPOSED FIX
  Implement `ddx bead ac-check <id>` (or `ddx bead check <id>`)
  with the following behavior:

  1. Inputs: bead ID; the working tree (or a specific rev
     range, default: merge-base..HEAD).

  2. Read b.Acceptance from the bead; parse into numbered AC
     items.

  3. Per-AC inspection pipeline. Classify each AC by
     lightweight regex/heuristic, then mechanically resolve:

     a. test-name AC (matches /Test[A-Z]\\w+/):
        - Run `go test -run '^<name>$'` on relevant packages.
        - Record pass/fail/missing.

     b. build/gate AC (matches "passes", "green", "lefthook",
        "pre-commit"):
        - Invoke `lefthook run pre-commit` or `go test ./...`
          per AC text.
        - Record exit code.

     c. negative AC (matches "no calls to", "does not",
        "remove", "no longer"):
        - `git grep` the named symbol on POST-IMAGE.
        - Pass if 0 hits (or fewer than baseline).

     d. symbol AC (matches `` `Foo.Bar()` `` or similar
        backtick-quoted identifier):
        - `git grep` the symbol; require ≥1 hit AND at least
          one diff hunk touching it.

     e. mechanical AC (matches "rename", "doc", "comment"):
        - File existence / rename detection via `git
          diff --name-status`.

     f. prose AC (everything else):
        - Emit `needs-judgment=true`. NEVER auto-pass, NEVER
          auto-fail.

  4. ACAliases support. The bead body may include an optional
     `ACAliases:` map (e.g., `AC #3: TestFooBar -> TestFoo/bar`)
     so a legitimate refactor can declare its substitutions.
     The tool reads it; the reviewer sees it.

  5. Output. Both human-readable and machine-readable:
     - Stdout: human-readable table per AC.
     - File: `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json` with
       structured per-AC results.

     Example structured output:
       {
         "schema_version": 1,
         "attempt_id": "...",
         "bead_id": "ddx-...",
         "summary": {"pass": 3, "fail": 1, "needs_judgment": 1},
         "items": [
           {"ac": 1, "kind": "test-name", "name": "TestFoo",
            "result": "pass", "evidence": "package x; test ran 0.04s"},
           {"ac": 4, "kind": "symbol", "name": "RemovedExecutionDirs",
            "result": "fail",
            "evidence": "grep found 0 hits; AC requires symbol present"},
           {"ac": 3, "kind": "prose",
            "result": "needs_judgment",
            "evidence": "no mechanical anchor; defer to reviewer"}
         ]
       }

  6. CLI: `ddx bead ac-check <id> [--rev-range <base>..<head>]
     [--package <go-package>] [--json]`. Idempotent. No
     side effects on the bead store.

  7. Tests:
     - TestACCheck_TestNameAC_Passes
     - TestACCheck_TestNameAC_Renamed_WithAlias
     - TestACCheck_TestNameAC_Missing_Fails
     - TestACCheck_NegativeAC_FlagsResidue
     - TestACCheck_NegativeAC_AbsenceConfirmed_Passes
     - TestACCheck_SymbolAC_PresentInDiff_Passes
     - TestACCheck_SymbolAC_NotInDiff_Fails
     - TestACCheck_MechanicalAC_RenameDetection
     - TestACCheck_ProseAC_NeedsJudgment_NeverAutoFail
     - TestACCheck_ProseAC_NeedsJudgment_NeverAutoPass
     - TestACCheck_JSONOutput_SchemaVersion1
     - TestACCheck_RegressionFakeRetainDays: replay the
       917cfbfa7 fake-work diff; assert ac-check yields
       multiple `fail` results for the missing prune logic
       and tests.

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the reviewer prompt or gate behavior (sister bead).
  - Wiring ac-check into execute-bead workflow (sister bead).
  - Strictness modes by bead label (sister bead).
  - New verdict types like REQUEST_CLARIFICATION (sister bead).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "fix(review): reviewer ratifies ac-check" —
    that bead consumes ac-check.json as the reviewer's primary
    input and changes the gate semantics. THIS bead is purely
    additive and can land independently.
  - Existing reviewer per_ac[] structure (execute_bead_review.go:204-234)
    is preserved; this tool produces structured input for it
    rather than replacing it.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/cmd/bead_ac_check.go (or sibling) implements `ddx bead ac-check <id>` with --rev-range, --package, --json flags.
2. The tool classifies AC items into: test-name, build/gate, negative, symbol, mechanical, prose.
3. The tool runs mechanical verification per AC kind: `go test -run` for test-name; git grep for symbol/negative; file detection for mechanical.
4. Prose AC items always result in `needs-judgment` — never auto-pass, never auto-fail.
5. ACAliases declared in the bead body (e.g., `AC #1: TestFoo -> TestFoo/bar`) are honored.
6. The tool emits `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json` with schema_version=1 and structured per-AC items.
7. The tool also prints a human-readable table to stdout.
8. TestACCheck_TestNameAC_Passes, TestACCheck_TestNameAC_Renamed_WithAlias, TestACCheck_TestNameAC_Missing_Fails, TestACCheck_NegativeAC_FlagsResidue, TestACCheck_NegativeAC_AbsenceConfirmed_Passes, TestACCheck_SymbolAC_PresentInDiff_Passes, TestACCheck_SymbolAC_NotInDiff_Fails, TestACCheck_MechanicalAC_RenameDetection, TestACCheck_ProseAC_NeedsJudgment_NeverAutoFail, TestACCheck_ProseAC_NeedsJudgment_NeverAutoPass, TestACCheck_JSONOutput_SchemaVersion1, TestACCheck_RegressionFakeRetainDays all pass.
9. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:review, kind:fix, observed-failure, prevention, spec:ADR-024</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="89946c262f30a0de91b9cd231339235438e4127d" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T233540-1514ca02"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit landed clean. All 10 ACs are satisfied:

- **AC #1** — `cli/cmd/bead_ac_check.go` with `--rev-range`, `--package`, `--attempt-id`, `--json` flags
- **AC #2** — classifies into test-name, build-gate, negative, symbol, mechanical, prose  
- **AC #3** — `go test -run` for test-name; git grep for symbol/negative; file-path detection via `FileInfo` hook for mechanical
- **AC #4** — prose always → `needs_judgment`
- **AC #5** — `ParseACAliases` added; `bead_ac_check.go` populates `ctx.Aliases` from `b.Description` + `b.Notes`
- **AC #6** — writes `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json` with `schema_version=1`
- **AC #7** — `WriteHuman` prints table to stdout
- **AC #8** — all 12 named tests pass including the new `TestACCheck_MechanicalAC_RenameDetection`
- **AC #9** — `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` green
- **AC #10** — `lefthook run pre-commit` passes
